factors.summary: The company is streamlining operations, incurring costs, but expects annual savings. Savings will be partially reinvested in growth initiatives.

evidence.text: Management expects to incur production transfer expenses and additional costs associated with these streamlining actions totaling approximately $1.3 during the fourth quarter of fiscal 2015. While management expects to achieve annual savings in fiscal 2015 in excess of these costs, management plans to reinvest a portion of these savings over the next twelve months in additional growth initiatives which require resources for further innovation, including talent with different skill sets.

factors.summary: Management maintains a positive outlook with expectations for solid profitable growth, outperforming served markets, and successful integration of new acquisitions.

evidence.text: Management remains optimistic about the opportunities for solid profitable growth for fiscal 2015 and the foreseeable future and expects that the Company will be able to outperform the markets it serves.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include potential rising input costs, commodity price volatility, and economic conditions affecting demand.

evidence.text: While some commodity costs have waned recently, others continue to rise. As the economy improves, management believes there is the potential for rising input costs. [...] The lighting industry continues to experience some volatility with respect to input costs. [...] Management expects that the Company will be able to outperform the markets it serves.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategic direction focused on addressing growing markets driven by energy and environmental concerns.

evidence.text: From a longer term perspective, management expects that its addressable markets will experience solid growth over the next decade, particularly as energy and environmental concerns come to the forefront. Management remains positive about the future prospects of the Company and its ability to continue to outperform the markets it serves.

factors.summary: The company is focused on gaining market share through new product introductions, expansion into new geographies, and leveraging its industry-leading portfolio.

evidence.text: Management currently believes that the Company will benefit from [...] further expansion in underpenetrated geographies and channels, and growth from the introduction of new lighting products and solutions.

factors.summary: The competitive environment is intensifying with new entrants and aggressive rivals, but the company is differentiating itself through innovation and market leadership.

evidence.text: The lighting industry continues to experience some volatility with respect to input costs. [...] Management expects that the Company will be able to outperform the markets it serves.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Sales volume was higher across most product categories and key sales channels. Sales of LED-based products increased approximately 55% compared to the year-ago period and represented more than 45% of total net sales.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2015 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2014

factors.summary: Pricing power is stable with insignificant price/mix impact on revenue growth.

evidence.text: The impact of changes in product prices and the mix of products sold ('price/mix') during the three months ended May 31, 2015 was insignificant compared to the prior-year period.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2015 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2014

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products, particularly LED-based solutions, which contributed significantly to growth.

evidence.text: Sales of LED-based products increased approximately 55% compared to the year-ago period and represented more than 45% of total net sales. Gross profit was higher than the prior-year period due primarily to additional contribution on higher net sales, a richer mix of products sold, lower material and component costs, and improved manufacturing productivity.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2015 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2014

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to increase, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: The Company expects to invest approximately two and a half percent of net sales during fiscal 2015, of which $42.3 had been invested as of May 31, 2015, primarily for equipment, tooling, facility enhancements, as well as new and enhanced information technology capabilities.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, with positive cash flow from operations.

evidence.text: The Company generated $158.2 of cash flow from operating activities during the nine months ended May 31, 2015 compared with $128.8 in the prior-year period, an increase of $29.4, due primarily to higher net income partially offset by higher operating working capital requirements.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable with total debt remaining unchanged and the debt to total capitalization ratio improving.

evidence.text: Total debt outstanding of $353.7 remained substantially unchanged from August 31, 2014. The debt to total capitalization ratio was 21.2% and 23.3% at May 31, 2015 and August 31, 2014, respectively.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing positive organic revenue growth, which is accelerating due to higher sales volumes and increased net sales. This growth is supported by market opportunities and product expansion, as per management's strategic execution.

evidence.text: Organic revenue growth is expected to continue, supported by market growth opportunities and product portfolio expansion.

Net sales for the three months ended May 31, 2015 increased 13.2% compared with the prior-year period due primarily to a 14% increase in sales volume partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency rate changes of approximately 1%
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2015 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2014

factors.summary: The company is observing mixed trends in input costs, with some commodities declining while others are rising. This volatility is expected to continue, potentially leading to future increases as the economy improves.

evidence.text: The lighting industry continues to experience some volatility with respect to input costs. While some commodity costs have waned recently, others continue to rise. As the economy improves, management believes there is the potential for rising input costs.

Gross profit margin increased 290 basis points to 43.2% for the three months ended May 31, 2015 compared with 40.3% in the prior-year period. These benefits were partially offset by an unfavorable impact from foreign currency exchange rate changes.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2015 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2014

factors.summary: The company is enhancing shareholder returns through a combination of increased dividend payments and stock repurchase programs, indicating confidence in its financial position.

evidence.text: On June 26, 2015, the Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.13 per share, payable on August 3, 2015, to stockholders of record on July 20, 2015.

The Company paid dividends on its common stock of $17.0 and $16.9 ($0.39 per share) during the nine months ended May 31, 2015 and 2014, respectively. Two million shares of the Company’s common stock are currently authorized and available for repurchase under an existing repurchase program. The Company expects to repurchase these shares on an opportunistic basis.

factors.summary: Demand trends are positive, with solid growth expected from renovation and tenant improvement projects, expansion into new geographies, and new product introductions. Additionally, there is evidence of accelerating demand as shown by higher sales volumes and increased net sales.

evidence.text: Management currently believes that the Company will benefit from continued renovation and tenant improvement projects, further expansion in underpenetrated geographies and channels, and growth from the introduction of new lighting products and solutions.

Net sales were $683.7 for the three months ended May 31, 2015 compared with $603.9 reported for the three months ended May 31, 2014, an increase of $79.8, or 13.2%. Sales volume was higher across most product categories and key sales channels. Sales of LED-based products increased approximately 55% compared to the year-ago period and represented more than 45% of total net sales.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2015 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2014

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins have shown consistent expansion throughout the first nine months and third quarter of fiscal 2015, driven by factors such as higher net sales, lower material costs, improved manufacturing productivity, and enhanced operating efficiency.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin increased to 42.3% for the nine months ended May 31, 2015 compared with 40.3% in the prior-year period. The increase in gross profit margin was due primarily to additional contribution on higher net sales, lower material and component costs, and improved manufacturing productivity.
evidence.subsection: First Nine Months of Fiscal 2015 Compared with First Nine Months of Fiscal 2014

evidence.text: Gross profit margin increased 290 basis points to 43.2% for the three months ended May 31, 2015 compared with 40.3% in the prior-year period. Operating profit for the third quarter of fiscal 2015 was $99.2 compared with $72.6 for the prior-year period, an increase of $26.6, or 36.6%. Adjusted operating profit margin increased 290 basis points to 14.8% for the third quarter of fiscal 2015 compared with 11.9% in the year-ago period.
evidence.subsection: Third Quarter of Fiscal 2015 Compared with Third Quarter of Fiscal 2014
